$schema-note: Bootstrap payload seeded into the AgentPolicy / CopilotSettings tables on first install. Override at runtime with AGENT_POLICY_BOOTSTRAP_PATH. level values: 'agentType' | 'provider' | 'model'. providerID/modelID rules: level=agentType keeps both empty; level=provider keeps modelID empty; level=model sets all three. Priorities are advisory — higher priority surfaces earlier in the copilot prompt.

policies.hint: Primary workhorse: filesystem, terminal, web-capable agents. Use the model hints below: stack ranked for complex work (prefer opencode-go/kimi-k2.6 first), for fast/basic tasks (minimax highspeed or zai turbo family), and for visual/image-heavy tasks (ollama gemini-3-flash-preview or zai glm-5v-turbo). Spin parallel agent_step branches only when the user asks for concurrent exploration or multi-arm verification.

Fastest IDE-style surface when latency matters more than terminal automation. Prefer providerID=cursor modelID=composer-2-fast for quick edits, small diffs, and default 'basic fast' work; also acceptable as a lighter fallback when opencode is overkill.
Preferred stack for heavy reasoning on complex tasks (Kimi 2.6 route).
High-speed OpenCode lane when you need web/tooling plus very low latency.
GLM family: use glm-5.1 / turbo variants for parallel arms or fast text; glm-5v-turbo for vision.
Good for multimodal previews (gemini-3-flash) and extra parallel Kimi/GLM arms.
Reserve gpt-5.4 when you need a third distinct parallel opinion on complex work.
Composer models — start with composer-2-fast for minimal latency.
FIRST choice for complex tasks: deepest Kimi stack on opencode-go.
SECOND for complex tasks: same Kimi generation via OAuth when opencode-go path is unavailable.
THIRD for complex tasks: spin up as a parallel arm when you need an independent GLM trajectory (multi-agent / verify another path).
FOURTH for complex tasks: Kimi k2p6 coding lane.
FIFTH for complex tasks: cloud Kimi arm (good for another parallel worker).
SIXTH for complex tasks: extra GLM parallel via ollama-cloud.
SEVENTH for complex tasks: third parallel / tie-breaker opinion when two Kimi/GLM arms disagree or you need GPT-shaped reasoning.
FASTEST Cursor lane: default for basic low-latency tasks; also acceptable as a light fallback for simple visual/chat edits when you should not spin up OpenCode multimodal stacks.
When you must stay on OpenCode but still move quickly — especially if browsing the web or running tools with tight iteration loops.
Fast ZAI vision-capable turbo: use as a fast lane for basic tasks that may glance at images, and as a cost-efficient visual path when you don't need the full Gemini preview arm.
Fast text-only turbo lane on ZAI when glm-5v-turbo is unnecessary.
BEST for full visual understanding on OpenCode (screenshots, UI mocks, diagrams).
